title: Implement Queue Using Two Stacks
description:
Implement a first-in-first-out (FIFO) queue using only two stacks.

The queue should support:
- enqueue
- dequeue
- peek
- empty
hint: Use one stack for adding elements and another stack for removing elements.

title: Recent Calls
description:
Create a data structure that counts the number of recent requests received within the last 3000 milliseconds.

Example:
Input:
ping(1)
ping(100)
ping(3001)

Output:
1,2,3
hint: A queue works well because older timestamps can be removed from the front.

title: Sliding Window Maximum
description:
Given an array and window size k, return the maximum value in each sliding window.

Example:
Input:
nums=[1,3,-1,-3,5,3,6,7], k=3
Output:[3,3,5,5,6,7]
hint: Use a monotonic deque to track maximum values.

title: Rotting Oranges
description:
Given a grid where 0 represents empty cells, 1 represents fresh oranges, and 2 represents rotten oranges, determine the minimum number of minutes until all oranges become rotten.

Example:
Input:
[[2,1,1],[1,1,0],[0,1,1]]
Output:
4
hint: Use multi-source BFS starting from all rotten oranges.

title: Design Circular Queue
description: Design a circular queue data structure that supports enqueue, dequeue, front, rear, and checking if the queue is empty or full.
hint: Use modular arithmetic to wrap indexes around the queue.

title: Shortest Path in Binary Matrix
description: Find the shortest path from the top-left corner to the bottom-right corner of a binary matrix.
hint: Use Breadth First Search because it explores shortest paths first.
